**Why no handoff brief was produced:**

Bird says starter limit = **3**. MJ says `STARTER_LIMIT = **5**`. These are not a rounding difference or an ambiguous overlap — they are contradictory constants that would produce directly opposing test outcomes on AC1. Sending Shaq a brief with this conflict unresolved would result in implementation of the wrong limit, with tests either passing against the wrong value or failing unexpectedly.

The professional tier (20) is clean and consistent across both agents — that portion could be implemented safely. But a partial brief covering only professional tier would be more dangerous than no brief, because Shaq might fill in the starter limit gap from whichever source he reads first.

**Recommended next step:** Route back to Bird and MJ together to align on the starter limit before synthesis resumes.
